revision.text:
[[ja:千葉県立房総のむら]] {{Infobox museum | name = Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura
Experience Museum Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura | image = Fudokinooka-siryokan.JPG | imagesize = 280 | alt = | caption = Fudoki-no-Oka Exhibition Hall {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|frame-width=280|type=point|marker=museum|qq=}} | pushpin_map = Japan Chiba | map_size = 200 | former_name = | established = {{ill|1986|en|1986|qid=Q2430|lt=1986|1=1986}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 61) {{ill|November 1|en|November 1|qid=Q2997|lt=November 1|1=November 1}}
(official opening){{Cite news |title=Boso-no-Mura fully opens this month |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=12 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1986-11-08|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inba District|en|Inba District|qid=Q677245|lt=Inba District|1=Inba District}}, {{ill|Sakae, Chiba|en|Sakae, Chiba|qid=Q1202754|lt=Sakae Town|1=Sakae, Chiba}} [[Ryūkaku-ji (Sakae, Chiba)|Ryūkaku-ji]] 1028 | latitude = 35.822861 | longitude = 140.273167 | coordinates_display = inline | website = [http://www2.chiba-muse.or.jp/MURA/ Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura] }} '''Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura''' (千葉県立房総のむら, *Chiba Kenritsu Bōsō no Mura*) is an experiential museum located in {{ill|Sakae, Chiba|en|Sakae, Chiba|qid=Q1202754|lt=Sakae, Chiba|1=Sakae, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. The site spans across Sakae Town and {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|qid=Q273798|lt=Narita City|1=Narita, Chiba}}, encompassing historical landmarks such as the {{ill|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|en|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11679371|lt=Ryūkaku-ji Kofun Cluster|1=Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkaku-ji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkaku-ji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, the {{ill|Former Gakushūin Elementary School Auditorium|ja|旧学習院初等科正堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gakushūin Elementary School Auditorium|1=former main hall of Gakushuin Primary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511028}}, and the {{ill|Former Mikogami Residence|ja|旧御子神家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Mikogami Residence|1=Old Mikogami Family House|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511057}}, all designated as [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|Historic Sites of Japan]] and {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Overview == [[File:Kiyuu-gakusyuin-syotouka-seidou02,narita-city,japan.jpg|thumb|Former Gakushūin Elementary School Auditorium (Important Cultural Property)]] The museum grounds are divided into the **"Experiential Heritage Area"** and the **"Fudoki-no-Oka Area"**. The former features reconstructed **Edo-period townhouses**, {{ill|Samurai residences|ja|武家屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai residences|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545899}}, and rural villages{{Cite news |title=Edo-period streets and villages: Experiential learning at Boso-no-Mura |newspaper=Yomiuri Shimbun |pages=Evening edition 6-7 |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=1992-04-10|qq=}}. The streetscape replicates the **late Edo and Meiji periods**{{Cite news |title=Visiting Boso-no-Mura: A hands-on museum experience |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=26 |publisher=Asahi Shimbun |date=1990-07-05|qq=}}, making it a popular filming location for dramas and films. Traditional skills demonstrations and hands-on experiences, including **martial arts, blacksmithing, and farming**, take place throughout the museum{{Cite news |title=Applause for traditional craftsmanship at Boso-no-Mura |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1992-10-11|qq=}}{{Cite news |title=Experience Edo-period life hands-on |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1990-06-06|qq=}}. The **Fudoki-no-Oka Area** includes one of Japan’s most significant **kofun (ancient Kofuns)**, such as the {{ill|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|en|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11679371|lt=Ryūkaku-ji Kofun Cluster|1=Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster}} and {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkaku-ji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkaku-ji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, alongside relocated historical structures and the **Fudoki-no-Oka Museum**. The museum also hosts **seasonal traditional festivals** like the **Dondo-yaki fire festival**{{Cite news |title=Dondo-yaki: Praying for health through fire |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1990-01-17|qq=}}, **Hinamatsuri (Girls' Festival)**{{Cite news |title=Hinamatsuri at Boso-no-Mura |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1990-02-24|qq=}}, **Tanabata (Star Festival)**{{Cite news |title=Making traditional Tanabata horses |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1996-08-20|qq=}}, **autumn festivals**{{Cite news |title=Edo-period canal restoration at Boso-no-Mura's Autumn Festival |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=16 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=2007-11-04|qq=}}, **Kyōgen performances**, and **street performances**{{Cite news |title=Kyōgen and street performances at Boso-no-Mura |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1996-06-15|qq=}}. == Fudoki-no-Oka == [[File:Kiyuu-mikogamike-ziyutaku,narita-city,japan.jpg|thumb|Former Mikogami Residence (Important Cultural Property)]] Established in **July 1976**, {{ill|Boso Fudoki-no-Oka|ja|房総風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boso Fudoki-no-Oka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千葉県立房総のむら|1=Bōsō Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406280}} was the **only** archaeological museum in Chiba Prefecture at the time{{Cite news |title=Boso Fudoki-no-Oka reaches 2 million visitors |newspaper=Chiba Nippo |page=14 |publisher=Chiba Nippo |date=1994-04-09|qq=}}. In **April 2004**, it was **merged with Boso-no-Mura**. The **32-hectare site** contains **78 Kofuns**, including the reconstructed **Ryūkaku-ji Kofun No. 101**, the **Fudoki-no-Oka Museum**, and relocated buildings such as: - **Former Gakushūin Elementary School Auditorium (1899) – Important Cultural Property** - **Former Mikogami Residence (1780) – Important Cultural Property** - **Former Hirano Residence (1751) – Prefecturally Designated Cultural Property** In **1991**, an **iron-making demonstration** using ancient techniques was conducted. In **1992**, the **Ryūkaku-ji Kofun No. 101** was reconstructed with **haniwa figurines**, accurately restoring their **original layout**. == Image Gallery == File:Merchant's Houses (Boso no mura) 01.JPG|Merchant townhouses File:Kazusa Farmhouse (Boso no mura) 01.JPG|Kazusa farmhouse File:Shimousa Farmhouse (Boso no mura) 01.JPG|Shimousa farmhouse File:Awa Farmhouse (Boso no mura) 01.JPG|Awa farmhouse File:Samurai House (Boso no mura) 01.JPG|Samurai residence == Related Articles == * {{ill|Natural History Museum and Institute, Chiba|en|Natural History Museum and Institute, Chiba|qid=Q9008805|lt=Chiba Prefectural Museum|1=Natural History Museum and Institute, Chiba}} * {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Museum of Art|en|Chiba Prefectural Museum of Art|qid=Q11406332|lt=Chiba Prefectural Art Museum|1=Chiba Prefectural Museum of Art}} * {{ill|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|en|Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11679371|lt=Ryūkaku-ji Kofun Cluster|1=Ryūgakuji Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|千葉県立房総のむら|version=102570724|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:千葉県立房総のむら]] {{Commons category|Bōsō no mura}} {{wikidata link|Q11406280}} [[Category:Museums established in 1986]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Museums in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:成田市の教育]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:日本のテーマパーク]] [[Category:日本の住宅の歴史]] [[Category:日本の公立博物館]] [[Category:History museums in Japan]] [[Category:栄町の教育]] [[Category:栄町の歴史]] [[Category:登録博物館]]
